statement: Instruction files that prescribe specific design patterns for specific situations without contextual heuristics for when to avoid patterns tend to produce over-engineered code — practitioners consistently report removing more forced patterns than adding them, and pattern choice should be context-driven not rule-driven.

evidenceRefs.title: r/cpp Design Patterns thread — pedanticProgramer: has refactored 10+ forced patterns out; cballowe: patterns are discovered not invented
The Pragmatic Programmer, Hunt and Thomas — advises against forcing abstractions before they emerge naturally from recurring code structures

publicSummary: Anti-pattern: instruction files that prescribe specific design patterns without teaching recognition signals or avoidance heuristics. Patterns should be recognized from existing code structures, not imposed beforehand.
